description.value:
Small-folk safety knots and house-signs — the Tonttu tie safety-knots into every object they wish to keep safe; the knots are invisible to those who do not know how to look, and the objects become inexplicably difficult to break, lose, or steal.

Effect: Once per respite, you can ward one carried object until the next respite. The warded object is unusually resistant to breaking, misplacement, or theft — the Director determines the exact nature of protection, but it should meaningfully complicate attempts to remove it from your possession or destroy it.

Rules: Director adjudicates; Respite.

project.prerequisites: An implement of at least quality craft; a genuine Tonttumál safety-cord tied by a Tonttu ward-keeper
